reviews.review_text:
Chapter 1 was very good.

Chapter 2 was really short, and nowhere the quality of chapter 1.

Now there's DLC before it is even finished.

Let's hope chapter 3 fixes it.

If cheap enough, Chapter 1 alone is a very decent size and might be worth it.

as my specs are low i have trouble playing most games but i can say that this one is ridiculously optimised, with my computer managing playable fps's at above minimal video settings and i would say that the video settings i play at are nowhere near pixelated or "non ideal." In my opinion while the second section is a lot harder on my pc it is still entirely playable and has minimal if any loss in experience when playing at reduced settings. overall the story is a little predictable at the start but i would say it is a good story.

On measure, for a game where the sexual aspects are displayed front and center on the store page, you wouldn't expect the gameplay to be as smooth as it is - because it typically isn't. For games like these, more often than not, the sex element takes precedence over, y'know, the gameplay - and yet here we are.  I can safely say I've had more genuine fun with this game than I expected going in.

The gunplay is as decent as it needs to be, for an over-shoulder TPS. The weapons run the typical gamut of pistol-revolver-shotgun-SMG-assault rifle-bow, but that's fine because they're all fun to use, so I was never itching for more weapons. Ammunition is rarely if ever an issue, so long as one manages resources appropriately, and once you get to the point in the game where there's vending machines full of the stuff for a relative pittance of money, you'll never sweat about needing more boolets, or bigger wepon, again. 

I haven't seen hide or hair of the weapon mods that the game keeps insisting are an element, yet, but that might just be me not having gotten far enough along to unlock the feature.

Regarding money: Up until you reach the first vending machine, you'll have spent some amount of time picking up strange red or blue crystals off of zombies with no immediate explanation what they're used for. Just before you find the vending machine, you'll suddenly start picking up actual bundles of cash. The crystals are sold to the vending machines for quick bucks, and once you learn this, the number of zombies who drop crystals and stacks of bills will skyrocket exponentially. In the same area that the first vending machine appears, there's even a room right across the hall where you can infinitely spawn zombies to shoot at for free money, so you can grind all you please. There [i]is[/i] technically a cap on how many supplies you can purchase at a time, but if you don't make a habit of getting hurt all the time, you won't blow through your healing supplies too quickly; and you get ten of whichever gun's bullets per purchase, and the stock is set at 500 for each ammo type, so you're not exactly on the struggle bus, here. 

To me, being able to carry as many bullets as I like is grand, but I can see how other people might feel like this mechanic cheapens the challenge of the game, since suddenly, you can just grab as much ammo as you want for your favorite gun and pretend the other weapons just don't exist, where previously, you would have had to depend on all of your weapons equally in order to manage your supplies. There are other vending machines which dispense clothing as well, if that particularly matters to you, so if you're not a fan of loading up on guns and ammo, you could just blow your cash on a new shirt or something instead.

Jodi's movements feel smooth and she's decently responsive to control inputs, unlike so many other games of this type where getting the character to move feels like hauling them through waist-deep quicksand at all times. Combat is snappy, with her gun snapping into position the instant you scope in on an enemy (or, well, aim down sights - I say scope in because I'm used to it). Her stomp move is as crunchy as its clear inspiration, Dead Space, and is more than sufficient for making sure any zombies you're not sure are dead for good don't go standing back up. Melee attacks feel weighty, and the automatic grapple counter Jodi employs if she has a knife in her inventory when an enemy attempts to grab her is such a breath of fresh air.

The movement controls might need a bit of rebinding for comfort's sake, because putting dodge on left control (meaning I have to rely on my pinky to avoid incoming attacks) is not what I would call one of the more intuitive choices. Jodi's walking speed is, well, a walking speed, but it doesn't feel obnoxiously slow, which is good, because as nice as her running speed is, she burns through stamina pretty quick. Knockdown recovery could be a little more brisk, I feel, especially since some enemies will just hover over Jodi and wait for her to stand back up so they can instantly smack her in the head again, and if you go down against an enemy with a gun, you may as well just reload because they'll mag dump you while you're on the floor. Parkour elements could stand to be tighter, especially given that some puzzles require some precision jumping and grappling, and I've run into incidents where sometimes Jodi will just do a pathetic little bunny hop instead of the running leap I needed her to.

The enemy variety isn't huge, but varies enough to keep you on your toes, and even the bog-standard "run at you and smack you in the face" enemies from the start of the game never truly stop being a threat in a tight situation. That said, once the game introduces the vending machines and ammo suddenly becomes far less of a concern, the devs fall into the tired old trap of, "throw enough zombies at the player to make them seriously question which country got depopulated to make this big of a swarm." Particularly the little facehugger-type enemies who simply appear out of the floor, ceiling and walls, giving the devs an easy excuse to spawn them infinitely whenever you walk into an area where they exist. Additionally, there is (so far, and to the best of my knowledge) one boss fight against an enemy who, if they manage to grapple you at all, will instantly kill you, regardless of how much health you have, or if you have any knives on you. This fight can be pretty annoying, even if you know what you're doing.

Certain mechanics feel like they're not explained terribly well, if at all (did you know you have to crouch in order to safely approach a proximity mine to use the mine removal tool on it? I didn't, because the game never said a word about it working like that), whereas other mechanics feel like the God of War devs seriously using DarkSydePhil as evidence that video games need to hold your hand at all times (try not to think too hard about what the codes to some lockers are. The answers will literally be right in front of you when you turn around. Not all lockers do this, but enough of them do to make it feel pointed). It gives a sense of peaks and valleys, where the devs were willing to trust the player to be smart enough to figure out some parts of the game on their own, but then they had a change of heart for other parts of the game and put the solutions on glowing neon signs, where giving the player breathing room to use their own brains would have been appreciated instead.

Given that much of the game takes place in an industrial complex with walls, rooms and corridors that are all the exact same color and design, it's not surprising that you might get lost while trying to figure out where in the world you need to go. I can honestly say that I made it as far in the game as I did because I [i]bungled[/i] my way there, rather than because I knew where I was going.

I'm electing not to speak on the matter of character customization, because that's a matter of personal preference, and my preference was to make Jodi look like she was, y'know, prepared to wade through a small army of zombies, so I didn't spend much time sexing her up any and instead had her wearing practical clothes that looked like they might hold up in combat scenarios (i.e., none of the especially revealing, lacy clothing options, because one would think that not having your skin exposed to FLESH-EATING ZOMBIES would be a relatively pragmatic choice to make).

So, there you have it. Even in spite of the gripes I've mentioned, the game is still fun enough that I'm okay recommending, flaws and all. I would maybe wait for it to go on sale, but other than that, if it resonates with you at all, you'll probably do just fine with it. As an added bonus, it runs decently well even on my potato of a PC, so as long as you adjust your settings appropriately, it shouldn't give you too much bother.

Yes, it's nice to have an attractive protagonist, but it's the combat and exploration that make this game compelling. The exploration, with the tension and anticipation of stumbling across a hoard of zombies, offset by some pretty tense and enjoyable combat are what make this game fun. The controls are smooth so if I'm injured or die it's my fault, rather than those of crappy controls holding me back. It makes it satisfying and rarely frustrating. Yeah, sure, there are some aspects to iron out but overall this is great fun.

The game is an 8/10 how ever it seems to be that the dev needs to add captions for those inner(brain) dialogue as they are in Chinese which i can not understand otherwise the game is fantastic so far, Although without some kind of quest map the game is also slightly hard to tell which location needs what objective completed and when.


游戏是 8/10,然而开发人员似乎需要为这些内部（大脑）对话添加字幕,因为它们是中文的,我无法理解,否则到目前为止游戏非常棒,尽管没有某种任务地图,游戏也有点难以判断哪个位置需要完成什么目标以及何时完成.

A polished, high-quality hidden gem for Survival Horror fans!

After finishing Bio Goddess, I was left with an absolute itch for more games that nail that specific blend of intense third-person combat, atmosphere, and character focus. Finding The Killing Antidote was a massive, pleasant surprise. It delivers a polished, fun, and greatly enjoyable experience that completely exceeded my expectations.

Honestly, I’m deeply impressed that this is an indie game made by just a couple of people. The level of care put into this project puts many larger studios to shame.

What makes it stand out:
 
Gunplay & Physics: The game feels incredible to play. The movement, aiming, weight, and reloading mechanics for the different guns are extremely satisfying and responsive. Combat against different types of enemies feels impactful, tense, and fair.
 
Stealth Mechanics: The inclusion of actual stealth elements is really well done. It’s not just about running and gunning; being able to crouch-walk, bait enemies, manage your noise, and strategically bypass hordes adds a fantastic layer of classic survival horror tension.
 
Puzzles & Progression: The puzzles are clever and tightly integrated into the environment. They force you to actually look at your surroundings, check notes, and engage with the lore rather than just mindlessly clicking through menus (the ⁠#520⁠ keypad riddle was brilliant!).
 
Personalization: The amount of deep cosmetic customization and clothes you can find through exploration or by solving hidden puzzles (like the hard drive decryption) adds a massive amount of replay value and personality to the journey.

Verdict:

Don't let the Early Access tag fool you. The game is incredibly stable, highly polished, and features an gameplay loop that keeps you hooked from the moment you start exploring. If you love tight third-person shooters, puzzle-solving, and a true survival horror atmosphere, this is a must-have.

10/10 — Massive respect to the developers!

This game is Spiritually RE3 remake but better.  It has Great fast responsive gunplay paired with L4D2 melee style combat.  I originally came to play a "Gooner Game" but I was surprisingly blown away by TKA's Action/Survival Horror elements and gameplay mechanics ( Just Imagine RE3 and Re4 but at 1.5x speed).   The Downside is that there are only 2 chapters to the story so far plus some additional game modes but that can be supplemented with maps and mods from the community which also increases game replay-ability.  I paid full for price TKA which might be a hard ask for some but this game does go on sale occasionally which at that point its a must have. With that being said, stop sleeping on these Asian Developers. The team at MetalStar Studio knows what gamers want when it comes to gameplay and "aesthetics" lol . If you're a fan of Resident Evil, this game will not disappoint.
This game turned me from a zombie killer into someone who can spend hours putting together a single outfit.

[h1] The Killing Antidote: A fun mess with lots of potential [/h1]

Honestly, I really want to love this game because it has [b]so much potential[/b]. The combat is super fun, gunplay feels snappy, and the character customization is surprisingly addictive. But at the same time, you can clearly tell the devs are a bit inexperienced with game design and didn't do enough QA testing before launch. 

[hr][/hr]

[h2] 👍 What I Liked [/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]The Combat is Great:[/b] Shooting and melee both feel punchy and satisfying. Fighting zombies actually feels good.
[*] [b]Cool Outfits:[/b] The dress-up mechanics are a huge plus and add a lot of fun personality to the game.
[*] [b]Good Foundation:[/b] The core gameplay loop is solid enough to keep you playing even when things get annoying.
[/list]

[h2] 👎 What Needs Work [/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]Clunky Tributes:[/b] The game tries really hard to copy famous Resident Evil tropes (like running from an invincible stalker monster or suddenly losing all your gear). But instead of feeling tense, these parts just feel kind of awkward and unpolished.
[*] [b]Weird Pacing:[/b] The difficulty jumps all over the place. One room is super short and easy, and the next is a sudden death trap with endless zombies spawning right on your face. 
[*] [b]Annoying Backtracking & No Map:[/b] There is [b]no map at all[/b]. Because every hallway looks identical, you spend way too much time wandering around aimlessly. 
[*] [b]Lazy Puzzles:[/b] Some puzzles are just generic math codes. Worse, others feel like artificial filler, forcing you to run back-to-back-to-back to the exact same room just to flip a switch or grab an item for no real logical reason.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]

[h2] 💰 My Verdict [/h2]
There is a genuinely cool indie horror game hidden under the jank, but right now it just lacks that final layer of polish. I wouldn't pay full price for it. 

[b]My Advice:[/b] Definitely put it on your wishlist, but wait to [b]grab it on a deep sale when it hits $5 or less[/b].

I honestly don't know what this game is trying to be.
Story mode gameplay is kind of fun. At some point I think it tried to copy The Last Of Us Part II with a random bow and arrow in a research facility, which makes no sense.

Visuals are nice, I like playing dress-up. The protagonist is, of course, gorgeous.

However, level design is too bland and unintuitive: every hallway looks the same and it's easy to get lost. I stopped playing for a long time because I couldn't get back to a certain area that had been closed off. *It really needs a map.*

I tried the other modes, and couldn't spend more than a few minutes on them, because I simply couldn't get into the mood. 

The story progression is bad. Just bad. It feels like it lacks heart. Just many ideas thrown together, hoping it sticks.

Maybe this game was made only for people who like shooting zombies and playing dress-up. I mean, I like that too... but that's it? Nothing else?

Hello Developer,
I enjoy the game's graphics and atmosphere, but several gameplay mechanics feel frustrating.
First, the dodge mechanic feels too slow and stiff, while bosses move very fast. It often feels like I'm fighting the controls rather than the enemies.
Second, the game includes many puzzle sections inspired by classic survival horror games, but there is no proper map system. This leads to excessive backtracking and getting lost instead of enjoying the puzzles.
Third, weapon switching and inventory management are not convenient during gameplay. Accessing and changing equipment can feel slow and clunky, especially in combat situations where quick reactions are important.
The game has great visual quality and potential, but the combat, navigation, and inventory systems need improvement to make the experience less frustrating and more enjoyable.
First and middle parts of chapter 1 are very good, mechanics are decent, but holy F[_]CK the boss parts are F[_]CKING HORRENDOUS AS HELL 7/10 good game overall

Honestly I had My doubts about getting this one but I've been enjoying it more than I thought, the first chapter actually surprised Me with good cinematics and plot twists, that being said of course it needs work, second level more of the same but the content is there and I cant wait to see what happens next.

Despite being early access, There is ALOT of content and gameplay that makes it feel like a complete game. I also really love the amount of extras they allow with workshop that you can add into the game
for what it is its a ton of fun, if you like other titles LIKE some titles that have EVIL people or doctors known as RESIDENTs you'll have a blast.

As someone who typically doesn't play games with a "fanservice" element, I find this game incredibly fun. I learned about this game through a streamer and was pleasantly surprised by how fun it looked. I'm even more surprised by fun it actually is. The shooting, weapon selection, movement, and puzzle-solving are all great. I'm finding myself eager to explore new areas and fight new enemies. Story-wise, it's pretty typical: an outbreak at a lab, zombies on the loose, etc. But there's enough of a difference here to at least download the demo and see if it's to your liking. The dress-up mechanic is actually a lot more intriguing than I initially expected, and I find myself looking out for outfits just to satiate my loot goblin desire.

All in all, a fun game. I recommend.
This game was better than I expected, although I wanted to start playing from the beginning sometimes.
Well, You can mod the game to your liking which is awesome, not only that, the game is just straight up awesome to play over and over, I can't wait for more episodes to drop it's gonna be a blast.
This game is simply awesome. The customization alone is great, the way it feels is also smooth. IMO its a great/ fun game. Would recommend to at least try for yourself.

I really enjoyed the zombie mechanics of this game.
The mechanics and story is good and captive.

I encountered one bug during my playthrough that required a game restart, but save system works great so no real progress lost.

The feature of dressing up your character seemed odd at first, but I ended up quite liking giving some personal flair to my protagonist, which was unexpected.

Great game, can't wait to see the future update brings.

FInal review score has to be 8/10

If you want an uncensored game, this will not for you, else it's a good game and if body censorship was not a problem for you it you can go on it. 

The game run smooth on old machine like mine.
good game, especially ep2. MC is beautiful nuff said

The Killing Antidote is a horror survival game in the vain of other well known titles in the genre. You play Jodi, a freelance reporter who is trying to uncover the origins of the virus while fending off the infected that inhabit the facility.

Gameplay: 8/10. Gunplay is great, like all horror survival, you have to manage your ammo stock by mixing it up with the infected with melee attacks (which can execute the infected if you stagger them). Traversal is on foot, sometimes you can climb on boxes to get over thing or onto a sea container, she can jump, just holster your weapon for more speed and distance. Seems to be lots of unlockable clothing items, found quiet a few on my run so far, a few other collectables and destroy objects objectives are scattered about the complex for you to find.

Sound: 8/10. Sound design is great, Jodi's VA is great, wish there was a subtitle option for the general quips she makes when attacking the infected, my chinese is rusty, so can pick up on the basics.

Graphic: 8/10. Even on my old ROG Strix G5 and the Steam Deck, this game looks great, well done devs.

I'm still finishing the first chapter, but so far there's a lot of gameplay. And it's a lot of fun sneaking around killing zombies (I mean, when is that not fun?) The mechanics are good and the visuals are fantastic.

For still being in Early Access it's one of the most stable games I've played, I don't think it's ever crashed or even glitched on me. And the developers still seem to be actively improving. One of my favorite recent purchases on Steam.
Even if this an indie game, it was sooo good! i complete the two chapter and play again in hard mode, it was fun, the gameplay and the feature.
A very polished and quality game. The story is good and layout of the different levels is very structured. keeps me on edge constantly, hard to stop playing. Can't wait for more.
really fun zombie killing game with eye appealing outfits. Everything about this game is really fun and good. I highly recommend it. If you're going for 100%, it does take a while. 9/10
very good graphics and gameplay is very fun

I will probably play this game again in the Future.

Why only in the Future you may ask? Well there is not much to be done Story-wise, I don't wanna go collect all these Gooner Cosplay Clothings, if I'm honest, if the Main character wasn't a Woman, I probably wouldn't have bought this game, but all that Clothing that there is to unlock is too time consuming just to drool over some cheeks.

 The story if you can't already tell is very Resident Evil inspired and well made, the shooting is well implemented, Puzzles sometimes are very crazy. The looting system as already mentioned, finding clothing can be hard, but most of them are just lying around, I also like that there are frequent Keypads to unlock which in order to unlock them you have to look around the Levels. Also what surprised me is that Clothing can be visually damaged so you perverts have something to drool on.(again)

I also noticed that you can change the Breast size and Hips, but not the rest of the Body.

If it's worth the 30$? Not so sure about that though, if you like juicy anime women, sure, but I'm not that type of guy so for me if there is more interesting Content it definitely would be worth it, but be careful the game is planned to be 1-2 years in Early Access and the game is out since 2024 and the developer wrote that they will increase the Price as soon as the game is out of Early Access, so I wouldn't wait too long, at the moment I just pray that there is going to be more interesting Content.

Yes good game, 100% and I am not drunk its a good game. At the beginning its mostly survival horror but once you unlock guns after the 2nd playthough its more of an action game. But the keycard models look like floppy disks.

一般
1.游戏人物衣服是穿模的，没有优化。
2.游戏设置里没有切换人物视角的选择（第一人称/第三人称）。
3.游戏设置里没有选项可以选择显示字幕或者不显示。
4.面对已经扑过来的丧尸，人物的手上或者背包里必须有匕首才可以反击
General 
1. The characters' clothing in the game clips through the model and is not optimised. 
2. There is no option in the game settings to switch character perspectives (first-person/third-person). 
3. There is no option in the game settings to display subtitles or not. 
4. In the face of zombies that have already lunged, the character must have a dagger in their hand or backpack to counterattack.

If you like beating up corpses for hours to make sure they're dead and have headshots do the same damage as hitting them anywhere else on the body (seriously headshots seem utterly pointless and disincentivizes having a good aim)  then I guess this is the game for you.

Very clunky, dull story that is just bland as the game environmental design. The titillation of the protag is literally the only selling point really and even that is meh at best, you can spend time with a game that not only costs less has better story, gameplay with an attractive character than wasting your time with this.

If you really have to play this game then I'd advise waiting for a steep steep steep discount, I got it that way and honestly I shouldn't have bothered.

The Killing Antidote shows promise, but the hype completely misled me.

Visuals & Atmosphere:
The lighting and environments are poor - that’s the kindest way I can put it. The lighting is flat and unattractive, and the level design feels extremely cheap and uninspired.

Character:
I’ve seen this character asset (possibly the “Xandra” model) reused in multiple games now. While it seems popular with some, I personally find it off-putting. The skin has a cheap plastic look, the proportions are awkward (oversized hands, unnaturally long arms), and overall she falls into the uncanny valley. Her movement is very arcadey - she glides across the ground with almost no weight or foot planting. The animations themselves are serviceable, but nothing special.

Gunplay:
This is probably the strongest part of the game. The shooting feels satisfying, though it remains quite arcadey in style.

Business Model & Development:
This is where the game becomes genuinely disappointing. Despite selling well, the developers continue running a Patreon and selling multiple paid DLCs on top of an Early Access title. A full year passed before the next chapter arrived, and it felt noticeably worse and cheaper than the first. Without the dedicated community mods, this “game” would barely rise above the level of a student project.

The whole predatory approach - milking fans through Patreon, paid DLCs, and slow, low-effort updates - is honestly disgusting.

As much as I want to like the game there are aspects that proved my patience very well.
The menu for the weapon handling is too clunky for m/kb players.
So i had enemys mostly (and repeatedly) killing you before i´ve been given the chance to get the weapon in hand that is needed to defeat them.
In addition to that I have a second complain: Because you are supposed to fall down after each attack from the tougher Zs, long before you even get finished with the standing up procedure (required for defending/attacking again) the opponent already starts hugging and biting, leaving the player in a hopeless position.
... and once again returning to the last savepoint (sigh).
I hope the UI and these mechanics will be adjusted in the final version.
As long as this is not fixed i can´t leave a positive review.
This Is literally the greatest Resident Evil Gooner Clone on Steam. As a proud Gooner I Can not wait to see what the Developers come up with next.

I like it!!!
It's like mix of L4D and revelations 1, theres good variety meele, range and charging enemies.
Also enemy is aggresive.
Gooner element is only mere a motivation factor.
Gameplay galore and way better than blockbuster genre counterpart.
Keep it up!!!

This Game is a scam. it boringly easy unitl the 2 hour mark then you fight and Enemy that you cant dodge , you cant run from and kills you in 1 or 2 hits. On top of that ther is no quick save so you have to run back from the checkpoiint  evert attempt., Its jenky and broken. You cant run , by the time you try to turn to see when to dodge, it hits you, and when it hits you you get knocked down and its wound up fo the next hit before you get up it BS. Game goes from very very easy to insane dificulty for no reason. It 2 hour in ffs. You look online for help and it the usual clowns giving usless advice and claiming its easy, so there no help just trolls. Sure you can turn the dificulty down but then the normal enemies are going to be even more boring cause you cant put the dificultly back up. I would guess the game is a mess after the 2 hour mark so your stuck with a game you cant return and cant progress in. I am so sick of modern unballanced games that spike the idficulty to insane level in one spot.
Great game so far, can't wait for the full release

اللعبة جدا جميلة تستحق الشراء ، القصة بسيطة لكن في الموضوع لا تتفرع الى قصص فرعيه او مهام فرعيه لتطيل عمر اللعبة فقط ، تسلسل الاحداث بالنسبة لي كان ذكي جدا بحيث مع هذه البساطة في القصة يجعلك تتشوق لمعرفة الحدث التالي والتقدم في القصة ، بالنسبة لي اعجبني ان اقاتل الزعيم الاول الذي ظهر في منطقة الاستحام في الفصل الثاني من القصة اي في المختبر ولانه بالفعل يحتاج الى مساحة للقتال والحركة خلاف ما توقت فقد كنت اتواقع ان اقاتله في نهاية الفصل الاول من القصة ، اللعبة في المجمل ممتازة وقد رأيت مجتمع اللاعبين يصنعون لها تعديلات ويضيفون ترجمات اخرى للعبة وهذا يدل على ان اللعبة حققت النجاح المطلوب لها ، اتمنى ان ارى من هذه المطور العاب في المستقبل مثل هذا ، العاب للتسلية والتشويق القصصي وليس لإرهاق المخ
